acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Update source-coverage-matrix.json after implementation with targetEndpoints, tests, parityLevel, and verificationEvidence.
If tests cannot run, record the concrete harness blocker instead of claiming test PASS.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: presentation/profile
requiredBehaviors: Profile.kt renders a static circular avatar from `R.drawable.ic_profile` plus an inert edit affordance, but it does not open a bitmap viewer, gallery preview, zoom surface, or EXIF/media metadata flow.

Profile.kt owns the profile navigation surface: the top-left back icon calls `navController.navigateUp()`, the logout button dispatches `ProfileEvent.PerformLogout`, and the logout callback navigates to onboarding while clearing the stack back to the graph...

Profile.kt keeps most profile content static, but it propagates UI actions through `ProfileViewModel.onEvent(ProfileEvent.PerformLogout)` and callback lambdas so logout navigation happens only after the view-model layer processes the event.

Profile.kt renders static profile content, placeholder card actions, and logout navigation only; it does not request runtime permissions, handle permission callbacks, or access protected platform APIs.

ProfileViewModel.onEvent` handles `ProfileEvent.PerformLogout` by launching a coroutine, toggling persisted login state through `LoginRepository`, and only then invoking the supplied callback so navigation happens after session mutation.

ProfileViewModel.kt contains only logout-event handling through `repository.toggleLoginState()` and a completion callback; it does not request permissions, process permission results, or touch protected APIs.
ProfileViewModel.kt has no init block, resume/pause refresh path, teardown override, or other screen lifecycle ownership; the file only reacts to a logout event when invoked by the UI.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.

implementationGroups.title: Verification and evidence policy
requiredBehaviors: Verify the phase's implemented behavior with focused tests, manual/emulator evidence, or explicit verificationEvidence.
riskHints: target tests only count as strong evidence when backed by latest test gate PASS or explicit harness blocker
build pass alone does not close per-feature behavior
manual review evidence must point to concrete target endpoints
acceptanceEvidence: Update source-coverage-matrix.json after implementation with targetEndpoints, tests, parityLevel, and verificationEvidence.
If tests cannot run, record the concrete harness blocker instead of claiming test PASS.

matchedGroups.title: Onboarding pager and progress flow
requiredBehaviors: Pager state is preserved within the onboarding session.
Final step transitions to login only after the last page.
riskHints: Accompanist pager is source-specific; do not copy its API shape into the target.
acceptanceEvidence: Page indicators and next button behavior match source progression semantics.
uiContracts: next action advances page or routes to login

matchedGroups.title: Login and logout session flows
requiredBehaviors: Credential entry updates local field state immediately.
Incorrect credentials keep the user on the login page with an error message.
Login and logout both mutate the persisted login state service.
riskHints: Do not replace snackbar feedback with silent failure.
Do not infer real authentication or network behavior that does not exist in source.
acceptanceEvidence: Target tests verify successful and unsuccessful login outcomes.
uiContracts: valid login routes to home and clears back stack
logout routes to onboarding and clears back stack
dataContracts: login flag toggles through the persisted session service

coordinationNotes: Prefer extending existing route/navigation/state surfaces over creating parallel shells.
If a shared surface must change, keep the change minimal and update only evidence rows tied to this group.
Repository/store groups should preserve existing data ownership and avoid duplicating global state.
